refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-001 (Refined): The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving/material handling tasks such that the cumulative productivity, measured in m³/h or ton/h over a representative evaluation period, meets or exceeds the customer-defined productivity_target, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to operator capability, task execution, and safety compliance. Refinement clarifies that productivity is a function of operator effectiveness (whether on-site or tele-operated), task execution fidelity, and safety-compliant operation. This frames productivity as a system-level emergent property dependent on multiple subsystems.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over the agreed evaluation period (e.g., 40 operating hours). Log cumulative material moved (m³ or ton), operational time, safety incidents, and task completion rate. Compare against productivity_target. Verify that no safety plan violations occurred during measurement.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute commanded tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with sufficient speed and precision to achieve target cycle times.
CR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable efficient task execution.
CR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) that do not degrade productivity below the target.
CR-NGHE-001.4: The teleoperation system (if deployed) shall maintain communication link quality and latency sufficient to sustain operator control fidelity and task execution rate.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Maximize vehicle hydraulic power and bucket capacity; optimize operator cabin ergonomics and visibility; implement predictive maintenance to minimize downtime.

DP-B: Integrate real-time task planning and autonomous task execution modules; provide operator with high-fidelity telemetry and predictive task completion estimates; enforce safety constraints via software governors.
DP-C: Combine vehicle performance optimization (DP-A) with intelligent task orchestration (DP-B) and safety-aware scheduling; use telemetry feedback to adapt task execution in real-time.

DP-D: Implement a modular architecture where vehicle capability, operator interface, teleoperation link, and safety services are independently optimized and integrated via standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link).
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Implement a modular architecture where vehicle capability, operator interface, teleoperation link, and safety services are independently optimized and integrated via standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). This DP enables productivity through decoupled optimization of each subsystem while maintaining safety compliance via explicit safety service integration.

potential_sources: Telemetry interface must carry sufficient fidelity (position, load, fuel, health) to enable both operator decision-making and safety service validation.
Tasking interface must support both operator-initiated and autonomous task commands without ambiguity.
SafetyAlert interface must not introduce latency that degrades operator responsiveness.
Network Link quality directly impacts teleoperation fidelity; degradation couples to operator interface effectiveness.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is modular by design but introduces interface coupling. Coupling is manageable if interface contracts are rigorous and latency budgets are enforced. Recommend interface specification review at next level.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) can be defined with sufficient bandwidth and latency guarantees to support both on-site and remote operation.
Risk: If interface latency exceeds operator reaction time budget, teleoperation productivity will degrade below target.
Risk: If safety service integration introduces false-positive alerts, operator will override safety constraints, negating safety compliance.

Assumption: Vehicle hardware (hydraulics, bucket, drivetrain) can achieve target cycle times without modification; productivity is primarily a function of operator effectiveness and task orchestration.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify interface latency and bandwidth requirements as part of DP specification. Define operator reaction time budget and map to Network Link and SafetyAlert latency constraints. Specify SafetyAlert false-positive rate acceptance criteria.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) and ensures that productivity targets are achieved while safety compliance is maintained. The NGHE Solution module is responsible for system-level performance monitoring, task scheduling, and safety policy enforcement.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits real-time vehicle state (position, orientation, load, fuel, hydraulic pressure, health diagnostics) to operator interface and safety services. Frequency: ≥10 Hz for on-site, ≥5 Hz for teleoperation (latency budget TBD).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands (dig, load, transport, dump, emergency stop) from operator or autonomous planner. Commands include target location, task parameters, and priority. Latency: <100 ms for on-site, <500 ms for teleoperation (TBD).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services (geofencing, speed limits, collision warnings). NGHE Solution reports safety-relevant events (constraint violations, near-miss, emergency stop) back to Safety Services. Latency: <50 ms (safety-critical).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for teleoperation connectivity (if deployed). Carries Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert over wireless or wired network. Availability: ≥99% during operation (TBD). Latency: <500 ms round-trip (TBD).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for CR-NGHE-001. It owns the productivity KPI and orchestrates all subsystems. Minimal diffusion: only interface contracts leak to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Vehicle module owns hardware execution (hydraulics, bucket, drivetrain) but does not own operator interface, teleoperation, or safety policy. Productivity is emergent from Vehicle + Operator + Safety integration; assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for operator effectiveness and safety compliance.

Teleoperation module owns remote operator interface and network link but does not own vehicle hardware or safety services. Productivity depends on vehicle capability and safety constraints; assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would diffuse responsibility for vehicle performance and safety compliance.

Safety Services module owns safety policy enforcement and constraint validation but does not own vehicle hardware, operator interface, or task execution. Productivity is not a safety service responsibility; assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services would create inappropriate coupling and diffuse responsibility.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 is a system-level productivity KPI that emerges from the integrated operation of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner because it orchestrates all subsystems, owns the interface contracts, and is responsible for ensuring that productivity targets are achieved while safety compliance is maintained. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that contribute to productivity but do not own the KPI.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution module boundary is not clearly defined, responsibility for productivity may diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, leading to finger-pointing and accountability gaps.
Risk: If interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are not rigorous, NGHE Solution may not be able to enforce productivity targets or safety compliance.
Risk: If NGHE Solution does not have visibility into Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services performance, it cannot diagnose productivity shortfalls or safety violations.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define NGHE Solution module boundary explicitly: specify which subsystems it owns (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), which interfaces it manages (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link), and which performance metrics it is responsible for (productivity_target, safety compliance, availability). Establish clear ownership and accountability for each subsystem.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (modular architecture with standardized interfaces) directly addresses CR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) by enabling independent optimization of vehicle capability, operator interface, teleoperation link, and safety services. The DP is solution-neutral (does not prescribe specific hardware or algorithms) and is verifiable through interface specification and integration testing. No functional gap identified.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution module does not have sufficient authority to enforce interface contracts, Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services may operate independently, negating the modular architecture benefit.
Risk: If interface latency or bandwidth constraints are not enforced, subsystems may degrade gracefully in isolation but fail catastrophically when integrated.
Risk: If safety service integration is not mandatory (i.e., Vehicle can operate without SafetyAlert), productivity optimization may override safety compliance.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module is the appropriate container for DP-D. It owns the interface contracts and is responsible for ensuring that all subsystems operate within the modular architecture. Containment is strong (score 95) with minimal diffusion (score 5). Recommend explicit specification of NGHE Solution authority and interface enforcement mechanisms.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-001 is verifiable through representative operations trials. Productivity can be measured directly (m³/h or ton/h). Safety compliance can be verified through incident logs and safety plan audits. Interface performance (latency, bandwidth, availability) can be measured through telemetry and network monitoring. Verification is feasible if NGHE Solution module provides sufficient observability.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution module with clear ownership and accountability. Sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-001.1 through CR-NGHE-001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Operator Interface, Teleoperation, and Safety Services respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Specify interface latency and bandwidth requirements as part of DP-D specification. Define operator reaction time budget and map to Network Link and SafetyAlert latency constraints.
Define NGHE Solution module boundary explicitly: specify which subsystems it owns, which interfaces it manages, and which performance metrics it is responsible for.
Establish interface enforcement mechanisms: specify how NGHE Solution ensures that all subsystems comply with interface contracts and safety constraints.
Define observability requirements: specify which telemetry, logs, and metrics NGHE Solution must collect to verify productivity targets and safety compliance.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a system-level productivity KPI that is not atomic. It depends on multiple subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) operating in concert. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-001.1 through CR-NGHE-001.4) that must be decomposed further to enable detailed design and implementation. DP-D (modular architecture) is sound and well-contained in NGHE Solution module, but further decomposition is required to specify how each subsystem contributes to productivity and how interfaces are managed.
decision.justification_detail: The FR-DP-IM gates all pass, indicating that the current level is well-formed. However, the sub-FRs are not yet refined or allocated to specific modules. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will enable specification of Vehicle performance targets, Operator Interface requirements, Teleoperation latency budgets, and Safety Services constraints. This is necessary to make the productivity KPI measurable and achievable.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute commanded tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with sufficient speed and precision to achieve target cycle times (e.g., 45 seconds per cycle for standard earthmoving task).

CR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness (camera views, telemetry display, task status) and control responsiveness (joystick latency <100 ms on-site, <500 ms remote) to enable efficient task execution.

CR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) via SafetyAlert interface such that safety compliance is maintained without reducing productivity below the target.

CR-NGHE-001.4: The teleoperation system (if deployed) shall maintain communication link quality (availability ≥99%, latency <500 ms round-trip) and operator control fidelity sufficient to sustain task execution rate equivalent to on-site operation.

assumptions: Assumption: Productivity_target is defined by customer and is achievable with current vehicle hardware (hydraulics, bucket, drivetrain) and operator skill level.
Assumption: Standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) can be defined with sufficient fidelity and latency to support both on-site and remote operation.

Assumption: Safety compliance does not require operational modes that are incompatible with productivity targets (i.e., safety constraints are not so restrictive that they prevent achieving productivity_target).
Assumption: Operator (on-site or remote) is trained and capable of executing tasks efficiently; productivity is not limited by operator skill or fatigue.
Assumption: Worksite conditions (soil type, weather, visibility) are representative of the evaluation period and do not introduce uncontrolled variability in productivity.
open_risks: Risk: If interface latency exceeds operator reaction time budget, teleoperation productivity will degrade below target. Mitigation: Specify latency budgets and conduct latency testing during integration.

Risk: If safety service integration introduces false-positive alerts or excessive constraint enforcement, operator will override safety constraints or disable safety services, negating safety compliance. Mitigation: Validate SafetyAlert accuracy and tune constraint thresholds during testing.

Risk: If vehicle hardware is not capable of achieving target cycle times, productivity target may be unachievable regardless of operator effectiveness or task orchestration. Mitigation: Conduct vehicle performance baseline testing before system integration.

Risk: If teleoperation network link is unreliable (availability <99% or latency >500 ms), remote operation may not be feasible. Mitigation: Specify network link requirements and conduct connectivity testing in representative environments.

Risk: If NGHE Solution module does not have sufficient authority to enforce interface contracts, subsystems may operate independently and degrade gracefully in isolation but fail catastrophically when integrated. Mitigation: Establish explicit interface enforcement mechanisms and conduct integration testing.
evidence_needed: Evidence: Customer-defined productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period (e.g., 40 operating hours).
Evidence: Vehicle performance baseline (cycle time, bucket capacity, hydraulic power) under representative worksite conditions.
Evidence: Operator interface design (camera views, telemetry display, control layout) and usability testing results.
Evidence: Teleoperation network link specification (bandwidth, latency, availability) and connectivity testing in representative environments.
Evidence: Safety constraints (geofencing, speed limits, collision avoidance) and safety plan compliance verification.
Evidence: Interface specification (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with latency, bandwidth, and reliability requirements.
Evidence: NGHE Solution module architecture and interface enforcement mechanisms.
Evidence: Representative operations trial results (cumulative material moved, cycle time, safety incidents, task completion rate) compared against productivity_target.
